feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: As AI companies race to go public, who else is along for the ride?
entry.content: Startups are trying to "ride that SpaceX IPO wave."

entry.title: The Guardian view on disability rights: the removal of legal safeguards brings risks | Editorial
entry.content: Charities are right to be concerned about the supreme court’s decision to lessen oversight of care settingsThe UK supreme court has ended a system of safeguards around the human rights of disabled people that has been in place for over a decade, in its recent ruling on a legal question brought to it by the attorney general of Northern Ireland. In doing so, the judges have alarmed charities and disability advocates and pushed a little-discussed aspect of social care regulation into the spotlight.Any person “under continuous supervision and control” and “not free to leave” the place where they live has until now been entitled to protections known as deprivation of liberty safeguards (Dols). These are part of the Mental Capacity Act, and include annual assessments. While the safeguards mostly apply to older people with dementia, children and younger adults with autism, learning disabilities and brain injuries are also covered. Continue reading...

entry.title: Charlie Javice Has Been Seeking a Pardon From Trump After Defrauding JPMorgan
entry.content: The entrepreneur sold her startup Frank to the bank for $175 million and was sentenced in September to over seven years in prison for fraud.

entry.title: People living with dementia are too often overlooked | Letters
entry.content: Readers respond to an article about how people living with the condition are fighting against damaging stereotypesAnne Karpf’s article is a powerful and timely reminder that people living with dementia are too often overlooked and underestimated (‘You’re treated like this is the end’: Meet the dementia rebels – diagnosed and determined to change people’s minds, 9 June). I particularly welcomed its emphasis on continued engagement through new experiences, learning, volunteering and social connection. Too often, a diagnosis leads others to focus solely on decline rather than on the person’s remaining abilities, interests and capacity for enjoyment.My wife was diagnosed with dementia in her 50s. While she was able, she greatly enjoyed volunteering, embroidery, singing, eating out, social events and participating in a walking group. These activities brought purpose, enjoyment, and a sense of belonging. They also demonstrated that a diagnosis of dementia does not mean the end of a meaningful and fulfilling life. Continue reading...

entry.title: Why diagnostic test waiting lists are so long | Letter
entry.content: Without investment in more radiographers, NHS waiting lists will continue to rise inexorably, says CEO of the Society and College of Radiographers, Richard EvansIn your article (Record number of people waiting for NHS diagnostic tests in England, 7 June), you quote Marlen Suller of Magentus saying that the solution to ever-increasing diagnostic waiting lists is for the NHS to “make better use of existing capacity, test patients faster, give them clearer information and use financial incentives to drive improvement”.This implies that diagnostic radiographers – the NHS professionals who carry out diagnostic imaging tests – are simply not working hard enough, or that they would work longer hours if they were offered financial incentive to do so. Continue reading...

entry.title: TechCrunch Mobility: SpaceX rockets past Tesla
entry.content: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

entry.title: Tech’s Next IPO Wave Promises a Charitable Windfall
entry.content: The SpaceX IPO is the first in a string of AI public-market debuts that could deliver new riches to startup employees to donate to charity.

entry.title: Can You Name EVERY WWE Champion?
entry.content: Can you successfully name every single person to ever hold the WWE Championship?

entry.title: George Russell Issues Stark Ferrari Warning After Hamilton’s Insane Race Pace
entry.content: The celebratory champagne had barely settled on the podium at the 2026 Barcelona-Catalunya Grand Prix before the reality of a shifting Formula 1 hierarchy began to sink in at Mercedes-AMG. While Lewis Hamilton’s historic maiden victory for Scuderia Ferrari captured…

entry.title: F1 standings 2026: Driver and constructor points, results for Formula One season
entry.content: A raft of new regulations promise to make the 2026 Formula One one of the most open and competitive for many years.

entry.title: Novak Djokovic denied Barcelona GP entry before waving flag for Lewis Hamilton’s first Ferrari win
entry.content: Novak Djokovic’s day at the Barcelona Grand Prix began with an awkward moment outside the gates but ended with him waving the chequered flag for Lewis Hamilton’s first win as a Ferrari driver. Even by Formula 1 standards, it was a unique journey through the paddock.

entry.title: Wings make roster decision on Paige Bueckers' teammate that thins out guard depth
entry.content: This probably had to happen.

entry.title: Hamilton wins first grand prix for Ferrari
entry.content: Lewis Hamilton takes his first victory for Ferrari in a compelling Barcelona-Catalunya Grand Prix as championship leader Kimi Antonelli retires.

entry.title: Raducanu loses Queen's final as trophy wait continues
entry.content: Emma Raducanu pays the price for a slow start as she loses in the Queen's final - meaning the Briton still has not won a title since the 2021 US Open.

entry.title: Backlash Is Growing Over Kevin O’Leary’s Data Center. He Is Blaming China.
entry.content: The “Shark Tank” panelist has criticized his detractors on TV and social media as tools of Beijing. Some locals in Utah say the tactic is backfiring.

entry.title: Nature: Hoh Rain Forest in Washington State
entry.content: We leave you this Sunday morning deep in the Hoh Rain Forest on the Olympic Peninsula in western Washington state. Videographer: Lance Milbrand.

entry.title: Early results indicate defeat of measure that would cap Swiss population
entry.content: The number of people living in Switzerland has soared by nearly one-quarter over the last generation.

entry.title: N.J. students' rite of passage via the Appalachian Trail
entry.content: St. Benedict's Preparatory School, a private high school in Newark, N.J., requires freshmen – many of whom have never been hiking or camping – to hike the Appalachian Trail for five days, with "some" adult supervision. Steve Hartman reports on an annual 55-mile rite of passage.

entry.title: Soccer art made from gum wrappers
entry.content: Artist Lyndon J. Barrois Sr. painstakingly sculpts art depicting soccer players. His medium of choice? The humble chewing gum wrapper. Correspondent Luke Burbank talks with Barrois about his Los Angeles exhibit, "Fútbol Is Life: Animated Sportraits," which captures iconic moments from nearly 100 years of the World Cup in tiny pieces of paper and foil.

entry.title: Jacob Markstrom trade rumors could help Oilers, Panthers, Sabres
entry.content: The goalie could be of use in a lot of different places.

entry.title: Former Champions League winning striker's move to MLS on the cards
entry.content: It appears that Robert Lewandowski's move to the Chicago Fire is still in the works.

entry.title: Former Ohio State basketball guard Mathieu Grujicic transfers to Radford
entry.content: Mathieu Grujicic will play the 2026-27 season at Radford after one year at Ohio State.

entry.title: Extended interview: JD Vance
entry.content: In this web exclusive, Vice President JD Vance talks with Robert Costa about his new book, "Communion: Finding My Way Back to Faith," his conversion to Catholicism, and a potential run for the presidency in 2028.

entry.title: Vance: Trump "very supportive" as vice president moves closer to 2028 decision
entry.content: Vice President JD Vance tells "CBS Sunday Morning" that he and his wife, Usha, will make a decision whether to enter the 2028 presidential race following the 2026 midterm elections.

entry.title: JD and Usha Vance on faith and family
entry.content: Vice President JD Vance and his wife, Usha, are expecting their fourth child. They talk about family; his book, "Communion: Finding My Way Back to Faith"; and his future.

entry.title: Extended interview: JD and Usha Vance
entry.content: In this web exclusive, Vice President JD Vance and second lady Usha Vance talk about JD's faith journey (as featured in his new book, "Communion: Finding My Way Back to Faith"); their interfaith marriage; and about expecting their fourth child.

entry.title: JD and Usha Vance on faith and family
entry.content: Vice President JD Vance and his wife, Usha, are expecting their fourth child. They talked with "Sunday Morning" national correspondent Robert Costa about family, his conversion to Catholicism, and his book, "Communion: Finding My Way Back to Faith." The vice president also defends the Trump administration's war with Iran, which has put Vance at odds with Pope Leo XIV.

entry.title: The Houston Astrodome: Inside the stadium that changed sports forever
entry.content: When the Houston Astrodome opened in 1965, the world's first domed stadium was hailed as the "Eighth Wonder of the World," and became one of America's top tourist attractions. But after decades of decline, the Dome now sits empty beside its successor, NRG Stadium. Correspondent Mo Rocca goes inside the landmark with those debating its fate, and those reimagining its future.

entry.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (June 14)
entry.content: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

entry.title: Passage: In memoriam
entry.content: "Sunday Morning" remembers some of the notable figures who left us this week, including "Today" film critic Gene Shalit.

entry.title: Knicks’ long-awaited title sparks wild celebrations — and a night of chaos in New York
entry.content: The New York Knicks ended their 53-year wait for a third NBA championship on Friday night, sparking wild celebrations across the city

entry.title: Europe’s Arms Race Is Creating Boomtowns. Why Are Locals So Unhappy?
entry.content: New investment in French and British towns brings jobs but sparks division and resentment.

entry.title: Iran says draft US deal includes oil sanctions waiver, nuclear limits and asset release
entry.content: Iran says draft US deal includes oil sanctions waiver, nuclear limits and asset release

entry.title: ‘It’s going to be extremely hot’: workers imperiled as sweltering World Cup temperatures are forecast
entry.content: It could top 90F in several cities hosting World Cup games – and workers could pay the price with their healthAs the World Cup kicks off, labor advocates and scholars warn that the workers making the tournament possible could face serious heat-related risks.“It’s going to be extremely hot, and you just cannot leave people unprotected or you’re going to deal with a lot of injuries,” said Jonathan Alingu, co-executive director of Central Florida Jobs With Justice, which has been calling for worker protections at the Miami games. “Or, God forbid, something even worse.” Continue reading...

entry.title: London startup to trial drug to prevent cancer therapy side-effect ‘cytokine storm’
entry.content: Poolbeg Pharma to test the treatment in NHS hospitals and says it is also developing a GLP-1 weight loss pillA London-based startup is about to trial a drug at six NHS hospitals that could stop people on cancer immunotherapy getting a life-threatening side-effect.Poolbeg Pharma said its oral drug POLB 001 could make treatment for blood cancer safer by preventing cytokine release syndrome (CRS), when the immune system goes into overdrive and attacks the body, leading to organ damage. Continue reading...

entry.title: The Faulty Logic Behind the SpaceX Index Trade
entry.content: There used to be a reliable additional bump from admission to indexes, but it has become less certain recently.

entry.title: A Guide to the Biggest Winners From the SpaceX IPO
entry.content: A look at some of the venture capitalists, college endowments and employees that hold shares in SpaceX

entry.title: Derry weren't good enough in Meath loss - Meenagh
entry.content: Derry manager Ciaran Meenagh admits his side have "no complaints" after their All-Ireland campaign came to an end with a 1-24 to 1-20 defeat by Meath.

entry.title: Active senior league soccer player arrested by Lahav 433 on suspicion of gambling, match-fixing
entry.content: The player's father was also summoned to provide testimony. At this stage, however, he is not considered a suspect.

entry.title: Roma not giving up on Fiorentina’s Dodo
entry.content: While all eyes remain on the big target of Greenwood, Roma continues to move decisively on other strategic fronts of the transfer market, particularly on the flanks, considered one of the key areas to...

entry.title: Team Liquid Alienware's "world-class" moments finding consistency ahead of MSI decider with LYON
entry.content: Team Liquid Alienware faces LYON for an MSI bracket-stage spot Sunday at Mullett Arena. CoreJJ and a red-hot Team Liquid look to carry their playoff run into the season's biggest match.

entry.title: New headstone for grave of Sheffield Wednesday hero
entry.content: More than £5,000 is raised to buy a stone to mark Ambrose Langley's grave at Burngreave Cemetery.

entry.title: Imminent departure confirmed for another key Arne Slot ally
entry.content: Liverpool’s Premier League title-winning head coach Arne Slot has left the club. Following his first season success the 47-year-old Dutchman struggled in his sophomore campaign. Slot paid the price ...

entry.title: Danhausen Is The King Of New York (WWE News)
entry.content: Danhausen celebrates the New York Knicks' NBA Championship win.

entry.title: PSG could swing deal for Barcelona rotation attacker in ideal contract situation
entry.content: PSG are being linked with a move for a Barcelona player who only has one year left on his contract as a potential rotation attacker.If you enjoy PSG Talk coverage and want to see more of it, add us as...

entry.title: ‘A huge spectrum of people coming together’: how parkrun made it to its millionth event
entry.content: Founded in 2004, the free weekly 5km event has grown into a global fixture of weekend life, taking place in parks, fields, seafronts and even prisonsThe millionth parkrun took place on Saturday, acting as a celebration of the community cohesion and public health benefit that the charity has been aiming to achieve across the past two decades.Those in attendance at the event in Bushy Park in west London included former Olympic champion Dame Kelly Holmes, as well as thousands of locals and parkrun fanatics alike. Continue reading...

entry.title: NHS staff battling wave of food supplement disinformation
entry.content: Exclusive: Cancer charity says dispelling falsehoods gleaned from social media is now routine task for cliniciansSocial media misinformation about the use of dietary supplements such as turmeric, St John’s wort and magnesium is now so common that dispelling online claims has become a routine part of NHS clinicians work.Two out of five frontline health workers say they encounter patients who raise inaccurate or misleading information about supplements at least once a week. Continue reading...

entry.title: Trial of 12mph bike lane speed limit grinds gears of Dutch cyclists
entry.content: Increase in road deaths amid rise of e-bikes prompts Houten to test willingness of freedom-loving cyclists to slow downAs road deaths increase and cycle lanes overflow with e-bikes, the Netherlands is considering a cycling speed limit of 12mph (20km/h).The government has started a two-week trial in Houten, near Utrecht, to gauge whether freedom-loving Dutch cyclists are willing to slow down – and whether they have any idea how fast they are going in the first place. Continue reading...

entry.title: Russia’s overwhelming manpower advantage against Ukraine is starting to wane
entry.content: Russia’s overwhelming manpower advantage against Ukraine is starting to wane

entry.title: Knicks win first NBA championship since 1973, beat Spurs 94-90 in Game 5
entry.content: The New York Knicks won their first NBA championship in 53 years after beating the San Antonio Spurs 94-90 in Game 5 of the NBA Finals.

entry.title: Just one game in, Brazil and Carlo Ancelotti are already under fire
entry.content: Brazil and manager Carlo Ancelotti are already facing harsh scrutiny after just one World Cup game, a 1-1 draw with Morocco.

entry.title: Nara Organics recalls baby formula sold at Target after multistate infant botulism outbreak
entry.content: Nara Organics has recalled its organic baby formula sold at Target stores and online due to a multistate outbreak of infant botulism

entry.title: A Housing Crisis Comes for Juneau’s Most Historic Homes
entry.content: Seven homes dating as far back as Alaska’s gold-rush era stand in the way of a plan to build apartments and condos to help alleviate a housing shortage.

entry.title: SpaceX’s Retail-Powered Debut Helps Steady Shaky Markets
entry.content: A crucial element in SpaceX’s soaring IPO was the army of do-it-yourself traders who bought some $118 million of SpaceX stock on the first day of trading.

entry.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
entry.content: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

entry.title: With uncertainty around H-1B visa fee, U.S. businesses unsure how to move forward
entry.content: Earlier this week, a federal judge invalidated the White House's $100,000 H-1B fee policy in response to a lawsuit brought by 20 states.

entry.title: Why Asian Currencies Are Taking a Beating, in Charts
entry.content: Governments and central banks are battling the effects of the energy crisis, U.S. interest rates and AI spending.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
You will live long enough to open many fortune cookies.
Lucky Numbers: 16, 91, 79, 65, 13, 75

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Use CTRL+CLICK to open internet pages in a new tab. SHIFT+CLICK to open in a new window.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: Grapes explode when you put them in the microwave.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Beverly Sills
entry.content: “Anger begins with folly, and ends with repentance.” — Beverly Sills

entry.title: Fans anticipating critical Game 5 of NBA Finals in San Antonio
entry.content: Game 5 of the NBA Finals is in San Antonio on Saturday, and the big question is whether the Knicks clinch or the Spurs survive. Game 4 was the most-watched game ever on social media with 3 billion views and counting. Doug Williams reports from San Antonio.

entry.title: Business owner says his company relies on skilled foreign worker visas
entry.content: This week, a federal judge struck down the Trump administration's $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas. The much higher fee was intended to prevent foreign workers from taking American jobs. But some business owners say the U.S. depends on that talent. Shanelle Kaul reports.

entry.title: Royal family watches Royal Air Force flyover for king's official birthday
entry.content: The Royal family gathered on the balcony at Buckingham Palace on Saturday to greet the crowds and watch a Royal Air Force flyover in celebration of the king. Holly Williams has more from London.

entry.title: U.S. cruises past Paraguay 4-1 in World Cup opener
entry.content: The U.S. men's national soccer team made easy work of Paraguay in its World Cup opener Friday, defeating the South American side 4-1 at SoFi Stadium.

entry.title: U.S. Poised to Lose Measles Elimination Status as Utah Outbreak Nears One-Year Mark
entry.content: The spread of the highly contagious disease persists at levels not seen in the U.S. since the early 1990s.

entry.title: OpenAI hit with multistate probe into possible user harm as IPO looms
entry.content: Several states are probing the safety of OpenAI customers using its chatbot.

entry.title: A woman's hypothermia death in Pittsburgh after her release from ICE custody is ruled a homicide
entry.content: The death of a woman from Haiti seeking asylum in the U.S. who died from hypothermia days after her release from federal custody was ruled a homicide by a Pennsylvania county medical examiner's office

entry.title: Trump names James M. McDonald to lead powerful New York federal prosecutor's office
entry.content: President Donald Trump says he will appoint one of his personal lawyers to serve as the top federal prosecutor in Manhattan after the attorney who holds the job was tapped this week for the role of director of national intelligence

entry.title: Trump names James M. McDonald to lead powerful New York federal prosecutor's office
entry.content: President Trump said Saturday that he will appoint one of his personal lawyers to serve as the top federal prosecutor in Manhattan after the attorney who holds the job was tapped this week for the role of director of national intelligence.

entry.title: The Iranian Enforcer Driving Tehran’s Hard Bargaining With the U.S.
entry.content: The pugilistic new paramilitary commander is seen as a power broker who is asserting control over negotiations even as the two sides appear to be near a deal.

entry.title: Indiana man's $100,000 lottery ticket validated at $20 in printing error
entry.content: An Indiana man who scratched what appeared to be a $100,000 prize from a new state lottery game was informed it had been validated at only $20, as the Hoosier Lottery acknowledged a printing error, suspended sales of the game and said it is reviewing how to resolve affected tickets.

entry.title: Lightning strikes plane flying from London to Charlotte, pilot makes emergency landing
entry.content: A plane slated to fly from London to Charlotte, North Carolina, had to double back and make an emergency landing after being struck by lightning.

entry.title: Trump Picks Sullivan & Cromwell Partner as Top Manhattan Federal Prosecutor
entry.content: James McDonald, one of the president’s personal lawyers, would replace Jay Clayton, who has been nominated as national intelligence director.

entry.title: Trump administration will not set nationwide environmental requirements, Zeldin says
entry.content: EPA CHIEF ZELDIN: No nationwide environmental standards for artificial intelligence data centers will be implemented by the Trump admin.

entry.title: OpenAI hit with multistate probe into possible user harm as its IPO looms
entry.content: OpenAI received a subpoena from several states as part of a probe into the safety of users of its chatbot as it prepares to offer stock to the public for the first time.

entry.title: U.S. agents at border near Buffalo find 1,600 pounds of marijuana
entry.content: Customs and Border Protection agents working at a border crossing near Buffalo, New York, found over 1,600 pounds of marijuana in a purported shipment of chocolate.

entry.title: A woman's hypothermia death in Pittsburgh after her release from ICE custody is ruled a homicide
entry.content: A medical examiner has ruled the death of a Haitian asylum seeker after being released from federal custody a homicide. An attorney representing her family said he expects her relatives to sue Immigration and Customs Enforcement in connection with her death.

entry.title: Mexican authorities discover body in trunk near Iranian soccer team's World Cup training grounds: report
entry.content: Mexican authorities discover body in trunk near Iranian soccer team's World Cup training grounds: report

entry.title: Unhealthy air quality in California city as huge warehouse fire burns for third day
entry.content: Officials in a Northern California city urged people sensitive to smoke to keep indoors as firefighters spent a third day Saturday battling a fire in a huge medical equipment warehouse.

entry.title: U.S.-Iran peace deal to be signed Sunday, Trump says
entry.content: President Trump said the Strait of Hormuz will be reopened "immediately," once the memorandum of understanding is signed.

entry.title: OpenAI faces investigation from state attorneys general
entry.content: It's not clear which states are involved, but they're asking about everything from OpenAI's ad policies to its handling of health data.

entry.title: Trump's name removed from Kennedy Center, court filing says
entry.content: A federal appellate court denied a last-minute attempt by the Trump administration to stop the removal of President Trump's name from the Kennedy Center on Friday.

entry.title: 6/13: Saturday Morning
entry.content: The White House is readying for Sunday's UFC event as questions remain over peace negotiations with Iran. Plus, the U.S. dominated Paraguay 4-1 in the team's first World Cup game.

entry.title: This thin under-pillow speaker helped me fall asleep without earbuds
entry.content: I’ve struggled with insomnia since I was very young. Like many chronic overthinkers, I tend to fall asleep best when my mind is occupied by something else, such as podcasts, YouTube compilations, or my personal favorite: rain sounds. But earbuds can be uncomfortable, and playing audio out loud isn’t exactly considerate when I’m staying at […]

entry.title: D.C.'s hottest food spot wants you to feel like you're at a dinner party
entry.content: Jon Sybert and Jill Tyler opened up Rye Bunny in the Adams Morgan neighborhood of Washington, D.C., after closing their acclaimed Michelin-starred restaurant, Tail Up Goat, when its lease ended. "CBS Saturday Morning" goes inside one of the city's most popular spots for an experience akin to a dinner party.

entry.title: A sportswriter and TV writer on what it means to be a sports fan
entry.content: Sportswriter Joe Posnanski and television writer Michael Schur were brought together by their love of sports, so they decided to travel around the world to collect more stories of fandom. The two sit down with "CBS Saturday Morning" to discuss their book, "Big Fan: Two Friends, 82,490 Miles, and the Wild, Wonderful Sports We Love."

entry.title: Full Interview: Jahmani "Hot Shot" Swanson
entry.content: Jahmani Swanson, known as "Hot Shot" sat down with "CBS Saturday Morning" to talk about playing for the Harlem Globetrotters as the organization celebrates its 100th anniversary.

entry.title: Metal grill brushes can cause serious injury. Here's how two best friends are changing that.
entry.content: Earlier this year, more than 13 million grill brushes were recalled after multiple injuries were reported from wire bristles breaking off. Firefighter Scott Mobley and Anthony Tranchida partnered up to create a bristle-free brush that uses steam to clean your grill, and "CBS Saturday Morning" gets a first-hand look at how it works just in time for Father's Day. (Sponsored by Grill Rescue)

entry.title: Harlem Globetrotters celebrate 100 wacky years
entry.content: The Harlem Globetrotters have been entertaining crowds with their comedic athletic feats for a century. "CBS Saturday Morning" speaks with current team members on what it means to be part of the iconic squad.

entry.title: Why TD Securities anticipates even bigger days ahead for SpaceX
entry.content: Peter Haynes, TD Securities' head of index and market structure, suggests SpaceX's public debut is only a small part of the larger SpaceX timeline.

entry.title: Anthropic says it has taken its latest AI models offline to comply with new export controls
entry.content: AI giant Anthropic said Friday it has taken its latest artificial intelligence models, known as Fable 5 and Mythos 5, offline to comply with a directive from the Trump administration to prevent their use by foreign nationals.

entry.title: Dow Jones Futures: The Next Test For Market — And SpaceX; Five Stocks In Buy Areas
entry.content: Dow Jones Futures: The Next Test For Market — And SpaceX; Five Stocks In Buy Areas

entry.title: Paramount Skydance merger with Warner Bros. Discovery won't harm competition, consumers, DOJ says
entry.content: An investigation by the U.S. Justice Department into Paramount Skydance's proposed acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery has determined that the mammoth Hollywood media merger is not likely to harm competition in the industry or be harmful for consumers.

entry.title: Alphabet Stock Is Up Nearly 100% Over the Past Year. Is It Still a Buy?
entry.content: Alphabet Stock Is Up Nearly 100% Over the Past Year. Is It Still a Buy?

entry.title: Some people are making guns with 3D printers. A new law seeks to cancel their print jobs
entry.content: Legislation in two of the nation's most populous states could force 3D printers to come equipped with technology blocking them from making guns

entry.title: From rockets to brain implants, here's a look at Elon Musk's vast empire
entry.content: Elon Musk, the world's richest man and now first-ever trillionaire, controls a lot of different businesses.

entry.title: From rockets to brain implants, here's a look at Elon Musk's vast empire
entry.content: Elon Musk, the world’s richest man and now first-ever trillionaire, controls a lot of different businesses

entry.title: My dad lost $250K of my parents' retirement savings to a WhatsApp scam he never questioned or verified
entry.content: My dad lost $250K of my parents' retirement savings to a WhatsApp scam he never questioned or verified

entry.title: Is Vistra (VST) Still The Best Data Center Energy Stock?
entry.content: Is Vistra (VST) Still The Best Data Center Energy Stock?
